title: Crank Scaffold Bindings Manifest
description: Single source of truth mapping abstract capabilities to concrete tools. Editing this file is how a clone changes its tech stack without altering the workflow or layers.

schema_version.description: Manifest schema version. Bump only on breaking schema changes.

clone_of.description: Upstream scaffold reference if this repo was cloned from one.

capabilities.description: Map of capability id -> capability definition. Capability ids are stable; tool choices behind them swap freely.

category.description: Grouping used by generated README tables.

description.description: Signal — the task that needs action. Becomes the row label in the rendered Standard Platform table.

ai_binding.description: Tool used by AI agents to deliver this capability.

human_binding.description: Tool used by humans for the same capability. Defaults to ai_binding when omitted.

install.description: How the bootstrap layer brings this tool into existence on the local environment. Omit for cloud-only capabilities.

last_checked.description: ISO date the version pin was last verified against upstream by the maintenance layer.

supersedes.description: If this capability replaced another, name the prior capability id. Lock-once audit trail.

version.description: Pinned version. Floating ranges (latest, ^x, *) are not acceptable per Standard Platform version policy.

method.description: How the tool is installed on a fresh Windows VM.

package.description: Package id for the chosen method (e.g. winget id, npm package name).

install_cmd.description: Optional override; otherwise derived from method + package.

verify_cmd.description: PowerShell command that prints the installed version on success and exits non-zero on failure.

pin_source.description: Where the version pin is recorded outside this manifest, if anywhere (e.g. package.json, infrastructure/vm/tooling.yaml).

post_install.description: Optional PowerShell snippet to run after install (e.g. CLI login prompt).
